layman_headline: Crypto market gripped by fear — no safe entries yet, staying patient for a better setup.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin slipping below $63,000 amid geopolitical tensions and a leverage flush. Most altcoins are bleeding, and the candidates on our watchlist are either wildly overextended after massive pumps (like DCR up 43% and DEXE up 77% in a week) or stuck in downtrends with poor risk/reward ratios. The few coins showing some technical promise, like JTO and ETHFI, don't yet offer clean enough setups with the right combination of oversold conditions, volume confirmation, and favorable chart structure to justify risking capital right now.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful and most coins are either extended after big moves or still falling. A better buying opportunity is likely coming in the next few days as fear deepens or a clear bounce pattern forms.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate has significant red flags. DEXE (RSI 77, +77% in 7 days) and DCR (+43.5% in 7 days) are classic overextended chaser traps. KITE has a bearish chart bias of -1.40 with a Bearish Marubozu pattern. VELVET's risk/reward at 0.26x is terrible. ETHFI has the highest chart bias (+0.60) but resistance at $0.40 is only 2.5% away with risk/reward of 0.11x — unacceptable. JTO offers the most balanced setup but its RSI at 59 after an 11.65% daily pump suggests it just bounced and we'd be buying into strength rather than weakness. BTC dominance at 56.1% continues to suppress altcoin performance. The Fear & Greed index at 28 is improving (from 24.6 weekly average) but not yet at the extreme fear levels (sub-20) where contrarian buys have the highest conviction based on our signal credibility data showing Fear_Greed as an 80% reliable indicator.
risk_factors: BTC holding $60K is now back in play per CryptoSlate — a breakdown below would drag all alts significantly lower
High BTC dominance (56.1%) means capital continues to flow away from altcoins into Bitcoin
Geopolitical Hormuz oil shock headlines creating macro risk-off environment that could intensify
watch_list: BTC price action around $60,000-$63,000 — a decisive bounce or breakdown will set the tone for all alts
JTO pullback toward $0.60 support — if it holds with declining volume, that would be an ideal entry
Fear & Greed index — if it drops below 20 (extreme fear), that's historically been our strongest contrarian buy signal
market_context: Bitcoin is testing $63K support after an Asian-session leverage flush, with geopolitical Hormuz oil tensions creating macro headwinds. The market is in 'Fear' territory at 28/100 but improving from last week's 24.6 average — not yet at extreme fear levels that historically offer the best swing entries.
context_banner: Market fear is building but hasn't reached capitulation levels — disciplined patience now sets up higher-conviction entries later.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) improving from 24.6 weekly average — trending toward contrarian territory but not yet at extreme fear levels where our 80% win rate historical signal triggers.
Bitcoin's $60K floor is back in play due to Hormuz oil shock — this macro risk creates downside tail risk for all positions.
Bitcoin ETFs drew $197M snapping an 8-week outflow streak — a positive institutional signal, but not yet confirmed as a trend reversal.
No swing candidate is in genuinely oversold territory (sub-35 RSI). Best candidate JTO at 59 is neutral-to-warm, and DEXE at 77 is dangerously overbought.
Bitcoin nearing Fidelity's power law support line tracked since 2015 — if this holds, it could form the floor for a broader market recovery.
